title: Calibration process for an automated test system
abstract: An example method, such as a calibration method, includes: determining a geometry of an arrangement of cells that is perceived by a robot configured to move devices into, and out of, the cells; determining an expected location of a target cell among the cells; determining an offset from the expected location that is based on the geometry that is perceived by the robot; and calibrating the robot based on the offset.
claims:
Claims 1 . A method comprising: determining a geometry of an arrangement of cells that is perceived by a robot configured to move devices into, and out of, the cells; determining an expected location of a target cell among the cells; determining an offset from the expected location that is based on the geometry that is perceived by the robot; and calibrating the robot based on the offset. 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein the geometry is a first geometry, and wherein determining the offset comprises: calculating a first dimension error based on a difference, in the first dimension, between the first geometry and a second geometry of the arrangement of cells, the second geometry being an expected geometry for the arrangement of cells; and calculating a second dimension error based on a difference, in the second dimension, between the first geometry and the second geometry, the offset comprising a combination of the first dimension error and the second dimension error. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the first dimension error and the second dimension error are calculated relative to the expected location of the target cell. 4. The method of claim 2, wherein calibrating the robot comprises incorporating the first dimension error and the second dimension error into a movement of the robot to reach the target cell. 5. The method of claim 1 , wherein the robot comprises multiple heads, and the offset is determined with respect to a first head among the multiple heads; and wherein calibrating the robot comprises calibrating a second head among the multiple heads that is different than the first head, the second head being calibrated based on both the offset and a second offset corresponding to a difference in locations between the first head and the second head. 6. The method of claim 1 , wherein the robot is calibrated to reach the expected location of the target cell by combining the offset with a perceived location of the target cell. 7. The method of claim 1 , wherein the cells are on one or more trays for holding devices that have been tested or devices that have not been tested. 8. The method of claim 1 , wherein the cells comprises one or more receptacles on one or more shuttles configured to move devices between different stages of a test system. 9. The method of claim 1 , wherein the robot is calibrated to a sub-millimeter accuracy. 10. The method of claim 1 , wherein the robot is calibrated to an accuracy of at least 0.1 millimeters. 1 1 . One or more non-transitory machine-readable storage devices storing instructions that are executable to perform operations comprising: determining a geometry of an arrangement of cells that is perceived by a robot configured to move devices into, and out of, the cells; determining an expected location of a target cell among the cells; determining an offset from the expected location that is based on the geometry that is perceived by the robot; and calibrating the robot based on the offset. 12. The one or more non-transitory machine-readable storage devices of claim 1 1 , wherein the geometry is a first geometry, and wherein determining the offset comprises: 

 calculating a first dimension error based on a difference, in the first dimension, between the first geometry and a second geometry of the arrangement of cells, the second geometry being an expected geometry for the arrangement of cells; and calculating a second dimension error based on a difference, in the second dimension, between the first geometry and the second geometry, the offset comprising a combination of the first dimension error and the second dimension error. 13. The one or more non-transitory machine-readable storage devices of claim 12, wherein the first dimension error and the second dimension error are calculated relative to the expected location of the target cell. 14. The one or more non-transitory machine-readable storage devices of claim 12, wherein calibrating the robot comprises incorporating the first dimension error and the second dimension error into a movement of the robot to reach the target cell. 15. The one or more non-transitory machine-readable storage devices of claim 1 1 , wherein the robot comprises multiple heads, and the offset is determined with respect to a first head among the multiple heads; and wherein calibrating the robot comprises calibrating a second head among the multiple heads that is different than the first head, the second head being calibrated based on both the offset and a second offset corresponding to a difference in locations between the first head and the second head. 16. The one or more non-transitory machine-readable storage devices of claim 1 1 , wherein the robot is calibrated to reach the expected location of the target cell by combining the offset with a perceived location of the target cell. 17. The one or more non-transitory machine-readable storage devices of claim 1 1 , wherein the cells are on one or more trays for holding devices that have been tested or devices that have not been tested. 18. The one or more non-transitory machine-readable storage devices of claim 1 1 , wherein the cells comprises one or more receptacles on one or more shuttles configured to move devices between different stages of a test system. 19. The one or more non-transitory machine-readable storage devices of claim 1 1 , wherein the robot is calibrated to a sub-millimeter accuracy. 20. The one or more non-transitory machine-readable storage devices of claim 1 1 , wherein the robot is calibrated to an accuracy of at least 0.1 millimeters.
